title: Regular Expression Matching
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” DP on Strings with Pattern Matching
coreIdea: Determine if a string matches a pattern containing '.' (any character) and '*' (zero or more of preceding character), using a 2D DP table where each cell represents whether a prefix of the string matches a prefix of the pattern.
recognitionCues: pattern matching with special wildcard characters ('.', '*')
asks for full-string match, not just substring presence
approach: Build dp[i][j] representing whether s[0..i) matches p[0..j). Base case dp[0][0]=true. For '*' in pattern, consider matching zero occurrences (dp[i][j-2]) or one more occurrence if characters match (dp[i-1][j] when s[i-1] matches p[j-2]). For '.' or exact match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1].
